title: [InstCombine] Simplify nested selects with implied condition
summary:
The developers generalized a pattern where nested selects can be simplified when the outer condition implies the inner condition. Originally, they only handled the exact case where the inner condition matched the outer condition (`TrueSI->getCondition() == CondVal`). The generalization replaces this equality check with `isImpliedCondition()`, which determines logical implication between two conditions.

This allows the optimization to apply to a broader set of cases, such as when `x < 0` implies `x < 10`, or when `x > 128` implies `x >= 0`. The patch introduces `simplifyNestedSelectsUsingImpliedCond()` which uses `isImpliedCondition()` to decide whether to replace the inner select with its true or false value based on the outer condition's truth value.

The key insight is that implication reasoning (`CondVal → InnerCondVal` or `¬CondVal → InnerCondVal`) enables folding even when conditions aren't identical but have logical relationships. This transforms specific pattern matching into a more powerful, semantics-driven optimization that handles various inequality relationships and constant ranges.
